item.title: NextEra bets there’s no such thing as having too much power
item.description: There are few obvious areas of overlap between Dominion Energy and its Florida rival

item.title: Baseball is learning to live with shorter attention spans
item.description: New rules have made games faster and more exciting

item.title: Tank maker KNDS comes late to the European defence boom
item.description: The Franco-German group might fare better reviving a previously rejected alliance with Rheinmetall

item.title: The UK must embrace its libraries in the age of AI
item.description: As stewards of vast quantities of data, the sector could play a critical role in fuelling the digital economy

item.title: Trump and Xi will not determine Taiwan’s fate
item.description: China assumes that the island would be helpless without American support — that is a dangerous mistake

item.title: Bonds bad
item.description: Plus, stocks bad

item.title: The battle the US is winning against China
item.description: It is a stunted financial power, leaving the US free to rule global markets

item.title: The Italianisation of Britain’s finances
item.description: Investors have watched UK political flip-flopping and budgetary shortfalls with alarm

item.title: The truth behind the manosphere’s ‘looksmaxxed’ interiors
item.description: The white, polished, supersized properties of Andrew Tate et al reveal something radical that even ‘The Matrix’ did not manage

item.title: Leaders’ judgment matters more than ever in the age of AI
item.description: A professor explores how to hone abilities too rarely nurtured in business schools

item.title: How to stay ambitious when there is no ladder left to climb
item.description: In retirement, traditional markers of success become murkier and structure must come from yourself

item.title: Asia is headed towards an industrial supercycle
item.description: It’s not just AI — energy, defence and industrial capex will fuel growth

item.title: The looming energy crunch
item.description: Supply squeeze due to the Iran war will get worse before it gets better

item.title: In America, wealth taxes mean wealth flight
item.description: California’s intended targets, whether individuals or companies, are leaving for friendlier regimes

item.title: The future of America’s military industrial complex
item.description: In an age of disruption, defence is undergoing seismic and lucrative change

item.title: Britain gets the politics it deserves
item.description: Cakeism, impatience and low-quality public discourse contribute to the UK’s instability

item.title: How Britain can tackle its mountain of unpaid energy bills
item.description: A long-term plan such as a social tariff could stop vulnerable households from running up debt

item.title: The disintegration of British democracy
item.description: We are witnessing the fracturing of the traditional party system that glues our society together

item.title: Europe’s leaders must stop self-censoring
item.description: White House pressure is being met with political flattery and strategic ambiguity that citizens find confusing

item.title: The many joys of small talk
item.description: Clever people have always understood the value of pleasantries at work

item.title: Gilts, not so bad
item.description: UK government bonds are not the outlier they appear to be

item.title: Jamie Dimon and his peers should beware of ending up on the UK ballot
item.description: There is a fine line between being ‘Concerned of Canary Wharf’ and becoming the subject for political discord yourself

item.title: How can Trump turn things around on inflation?
item.description: Most of the culpability rests with the Fed and the Biden government but voters will hold the president accountable

item.title: Tech titans should pick up the phone — and so should the rest of us
item.description: The lesson from Elon Musk’s case against OpenAI is that writing things down can be embarrassing

item.title: The century of the stalemate
item.description: In war, politics and other fields, it is ever harder to win
